title: DL-019-transformer-architectureеҺҹзҗҶдёҺеә”з”Ё

abstract:
DL-019 вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ What are Transformers? Explain Architecture and Applications

 Question
What is the Transformer architecture? Explain its components, how it differs from RNNs, and its applications.

 Answer

 What is a Transformer?
Transformer is a neural network architecture introduced in "Attention Is All You Need" (Vaswani et al., 2017) that relies entirely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions. It has become the foundation for modern NLP and is expandin...

sections.title: Question
sections.content: What is the Transformer architecture? Explain its components, how it differs from RNNs, and its applications.

sections.title: What is a Transformer?
sections.content: **Transformer** is a neural network architecture introduced in "Attention Is All You Need" (Vaswani et al., 2017) that relies entirely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions. It has become the foundation for modern NLP and is expanding to vision, speech, and multimodal tasks.

sections.title: Key Innovation
sections.content:
```
RNN/CNN/LSTM: Sequential processing or local connections
Transformer: Global attention between all positions

Result:
- Better long-range dependency modeling
- Highly parallelizable
- State-of-the-art performance
```

---

sections.title: Encoder-Decoder Structure
sections.content:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  DECODER                                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Masked       в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Self-Attn    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Cross-Attn   в”Ӯ вҶҗ attends to encoder output   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Feed Forward в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ  ENCODER                                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Self-Attn    в”Ӯ вҶҗ attends to all positions    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Feed Forward в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                               в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

---

sections.title: 1. Input Embedding
sections.content:
```
Tokens вҶ’ Embedding Matrix вҶ’ Vector representations

Example:
"Hello world" вҶ’ [101, 2054] вҶ’ [512-dim vectors]
```

sections.title: 2. Positional Encoding
sections.content:
```
Problem: Self-attention has no notion of order
Solution: Add position information to embeddings

Encoding:
PE(pos, 2i) = sin(pos / 10000^(2i/d_model))
PE(pos, 2i+1) = cos(pos / 10000^(2i/d_model))

Added to input embeddings:
Input = Token_Embedding + Positional_Encoding
```

sections.title: Why Sinusoidal?
sections.content:
```
- Allows model to learn relative positions
- Can generalize to sequence lengths not seen in training
- Alternative: Learnable positional embeddings
```

sections.title: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
sections.content:
```
Attention(Q, K, V) = softmax(QKбөҖ / вҲҡd_k) Г— V
```

sections.title: Multi-Head Extension
sections.content:
```
MultiHead(Q, K, V) = Concat(headвӮҒ, ..., headвӮ•) Г— W_O

Each head: headбөў = Attention(QW_Qбөў, KW_Kбөў, VW_Vбөў)

Typical: 8 or 16 heads, 64 dimensions per head
```

sections.title: 4. Add & Norm (Residual + LayerNorm)
sections.content:
```
Output = LayerNorm(x + Sublayer(x))

Where:
- x: Input to sublayer
- Sublayer: Attention or Feed Forward
- Residual connection helps gradient flow
- LayerNorm stabilizes training
```

sections.title: 5. Feed Forward Network
sections.content:
```
FFN(x) = max(0, xWвӮҒ + bвӮҒ)WвӮӮ + bвӮӮ

Two linear transformations with ReLU activation
Applied position-wise (same FFN for each position)
```

sections.title: 6. Output Layer
sections.content:
```
Final representation вҶ’ Linear вҶ’ Softmax вҶ’ Probabilities

For language modeling:
Predicts next token probability distribution
```

---

sections.title: Transformer Encoder
sections.content:
```
Purpose: Understand input sequence

Structure:
Input вҶ’ Embedding + PosEnc вҶ’ [Self-Attn вҶ’ Add&Norm вҶ’ FFN вҶ’ Add&Norm] Г— N

Attention: Full (can see all positions)
Use cases: BERT, encoding tasks
```

sections.title: Transformer Decoder
sections.content:
```
Purpose: Generate output sequence

Structure:
Input вҶ’ Embedding + PosEnc вҶ’ [Masked Self-Attn вҶ’ Cross-Attn вҶ’ FFN] Г— N вҶ’ Output

Attention: 
- Masked self-attention (can only see past)
- Cross-attention (attends to encoder)
Use cases: GPT, language generation
```

---

sections.title: Transformer vs RNN
sections.content:
| Aspect | RNN/LSTM | Transformer |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| **Processing** | Sequential | Parallel |
| **Long Dependencies** | Limited (vanishing gradient) | Excellent (direct attention) |
| **Training Speed** | Slow (can't parallelize) | Fast (fully parallel) |
| **Memory** | O(1) per step | O(nВІ) for attention |
| **Position** | Implicit (order preserved) | Explicit (positional encoding) |
| **Context** | Fixed (hidden state) | Dynamic (attention weights) |

sections.title: Speed Comparison
sections.content:
```
Training on same task:
RNN: 10 days
Transformer: 1 day (10Г— faster!)
```

---

sections.title: Encoder-Only (BERT-style)
sections.content:
```
Architecture: Encoder stack only
Training: Masked language modeling
Use cases: Text classification, NER, QA
Examples: BERT, RoBERTa, DistilBERT
```

sections.title: Decoder-Only (GPT-style)
sections.content:
```
Architecture: Decoder stack only
Training: Causal language modeling (predict next token)
Use cases: Text generation, chatbots
Examples: GPT-2, GPT-3, GPT-4, LLaMA
```

sections.title: Encoder-Decoder (Original)
sections.content:
```
Architecture: Both encoder and decoder
Training: Sequence-to-sequence
Use cases: Translation, summarization
Examples: Original Transformer, T5, BART
```

---

sections.title: 1. Natural Language Processing
sections.content:
| Task | Model | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| **Text Classification** | BERT | Sentiment, topic, spam detection |
| **Named Entity Recognition** | BERT | Extract names, places, dates |
| **Question Answering** | BERT, T5 | Answer questions from context |
| **Machine Translation** | Transformer | Translate between languages |
| **Text Generation** | GPT | Generate articles, stories, code |
| **Summarization** | BART, T5 | Create summaries of long text |

sections.title: 2. Computer Vision
sections.content:
| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **ViT (Vision Transformer)** | Image classification with transformers |
| **DETR** | Object detection with transformers |
| **Segment Anything** | Image segmentation |

sections.title: 3. Multimodal
sections.content:
| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **CLIP** | Image-text understanding |
| **DALL-E** | Text-to-image generation |
| **Flamingo** | Visual language model |

sections.title: 4. Speech
sections.content:
| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Whisper** | Speech recognition |
| **AudioLM** | Audio generation |

---

sections.title: 1. Scalability
sections.content:
```
More data + more parameters = better performance
Law holds even at massive scale (GPT-3: 175B params)
```

sections.title: 2. Transfer Learning
sections.content:
```
Pretrain on massive data вҶ’ Fine-tune on specific tasks
One model, many applications
```

sections.title: 3. Parallelization
sections.content:
```
Train on hundreds of GPUs efficiently
Much faster than RNNs
```

sections.title: 4. Performance
sections.content:
```
State-of-the-art on most NLP benchmarks
Expanding to vision, speech, science
```

---

sections.title: 1. Computational Complexity
sections.content:
```
Self-attention: O(nВІ) memory and time
Long sequences become expensive

Solutions: Sparse attention, linear attention
```

sections.title: 2. Data Hungry
sections.content:
```
Needs massive pretraining data
Not suitable for low-resource settings without fine-tuning
```

sections.title: 3. Fixed Context Window
sections.content:
```
Can only attend to tokens within window
GPT-4: ~128K tokens max

Solutions: Sliding window, memory mechanisms
```

sections.title: 4. Lack of True Understanding
sections.content:
```
Statistical patterns, not reasoning
Can make logical errors
```

---

sections.title: Code Example (Hugging Face)
sections.content:
```python
from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer

# Load pretrained transformer
model_name = "bert-base-uncased"
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name)
model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(model_name)

# Tokenize input
text = "Hello, I'm a transformer!"
inputs = tokenizer(text, return_tensors="pt")

# Forward pass
outputs = model(**inputs)

# Get contextualized representations
last_hidden_states = outputs.last_hidden_state
# Shape: (batch, seq_len, hidden_dim)
```

sections.title: Key Takeaways
sections.content:
- Transformers use self-attention instead of recurrence
- Fully parallelizable, much faster than RNNs
- Encoder-decoder, encoder-only, or decoder-only variants
- Foundation of modern LLMs (BERT, GPT, etc.)
- Applications: NLP, vision, speech, multimodal
- Limitations: O(nВІ) complexity, data hungry
- Dominant architecture in modern deep learning

sections.title: Tags: [transformer, attention, NLP, BERT, GPT, architecture]
